title: 1.1: Tests that BitExtendedField.i2m() returns zero output when encoding the minimum value (0) for all extension_bit positions
1.2: Tests that BitExtendedField.i2m() returns expected output when encoding the maximum single-unit value for all extension_bit positions
1.3: Tests that BitExtendedField.i2m() produces a multi-byte result when encoding a value that exceeds single-unit capacity
1.4: Tests that BitExtendedField.i2m() produces different encoded outputs depending on the extension_bit position when encoding the same input value
1.5: Tests that BitExtendedField.m2i() correctly decodes encoded byte strings back to the original value for all extension_bit positions
1.7: Tests that LSBExtendedField correctly decodes encoded bytes back to the original value using m2i() with continuation marker at bit position 0
1.8: Tests that MSBExtendedField correctly encodes a value using i2m() with continuation marker at bit position 7
1.9: Tests that MSBExtendedField correctly decodes encoded bytes back to the original value using m2i() with continuation marker at bit position 7
2.1: Places the test for boundary value encoding in test/fields.uts
2.2: Places the test for field expansion in test/fields.uts
2.3: Places the test for encoding with variable continuation marker positions in test/fields.uts
2.4: Places the test for decoding with variable continuation marker positions in test/fields.uts
2.5: Places the test for LSBExtendedField operations in test/fields.uts
2.6: Places the test for MSBExtendedField operations in test/fields.uts
3.1: All tests use UTScapy format with = symbol as test section header
3.2: All tests use assert statements for result verification
4.1: Encoding tests convert byte results to comparable format before assertion
4.2: Tests for variable marker positions iterate over all 8 extension_bit positions (0-7)
